name: Simulated Annealing (SA)
applicable_scenarios: single-solution metaheuristic for combinatorial or continuous optimization
problems with many local minima where escaping them is important
python_template:
import numpy as np

def simulated_annealing(f, x0, neighbor, T0=1.0, alpha=0.995, n_iter=10000, rng=None):
    rng = rng or np.random.default_rng(0)
    x, fx = x0, f(x0)
    best, fbest = x, fx
    T = T0
    for _ in range(n_iter):
        xp = neighbor(x, rng)
        fp = f(xp)
        if fp < fx or rng.random() < np.exp(-(fp - fx) / max(T, 1e-12)):
            x, fx = xp, fp
            if fx < fbest:
                best, fbest = x, fx
        T *= alpha
    return best, fbest
common_pitfalls: cooling schedule dominates quality; too fast → local minima, too slow → wasted compute
stochastic; budget multiple runs and pick the best
no gradient information used — dense continuous problems may prefer L-BFGS
